disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Over the past two days the patient pushed their physical activity far beyond their usual limits to keep pace with others at work.
trigger_question_examples: What were you doing in the days before this started?
Has anything been different about your activity recently?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've been driving myself really hard for two days to keep up with everyone else, way past what I'd usually do to my body.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Diffuse muscle pain with visible twitching (fasciculations) across both legs.
trigger_question_examples: Can you describe what your muscles are doing?
Where does it hurt and what does it feel like?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My legs are aching everywhere and the muscles keep twitching on their own — you can actually see them jumping.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Marked bilateral leg weakness — unable to stand, transfer, or safely reach fluids or the toilet.
trigger_question_examples: Are you able to stand or walk right now?
Can you get yourself to the bathroom or get a drink?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — my legs won't hold me. I can't stand or get to the toilet on my own, my roommate has to help me.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No change in urine colour so far (no dark or tea-coloured urine noticed), though the patient has not passed urine recently.
trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed your urine going dark, brown, or tea-coloured?
When did you last pass urine and what did it look like?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's looked normal to me, but honestly I haven't been able to get to the toilet much today.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The patient delayed their usual daily medication today while trying to work through the shift.
trigger_question_examples: Have you taken your usual medications today?
Any changes to your regular medicines recently?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I put off my afternoon dose today because I was trying to just push through work.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The patient feels their heart beating faster than usual and feels shaky.
trigger_question_examples: How does your heart feel — is it racing or pounding?
Do you feel shaky, dizzy, or faint?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My heart feels like it's going a bit fast and I feel really shaky, but I'm not dizzy or about to pass out.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The patient expresses distress and feelings of worthlessness connected to not keeping up, and repeatedly apologises for the inconvenience.
trigger_question_examples: How are you feeling in yourself through all this?
What's been going through your mind?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I feel useless — like I'm only worth what I can get done — and I'm so sorry for making everyone deal with this.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: On sensitive direct questioning the patient denies any thoughts of harming themselves or wanting to die.
trigger_question_examples: When people feel this worn down and low, they sometimes have thoughts of not wanting to be here — have you had any thoughts like that?
Have you had any thoughts of harming yourself?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I don't want to hurt myself — I just feel exhausted and useless, that's all.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I'm fine, it's nothing like that, please don't make a big deal of it.
